phrases.intent_en: Ask about the next show time
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure commence le prochain spectacle équestre ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UR koh-MAHNS luh pro-SHAN spek-TAK-luh ay-KEST-ruh ?
phrases.cultural_note: Equestrian sports ('les sports équestres') are hugely popular in France — France is one of the top equestrian nations in the world, and horses carry real cultural weight.

responses.french: Le prochain spectacle commence à quatorze heures trente.
responses.english: The next show starts at two-thirty.
responses.phonetic: luh pro-SHAN spek-TAK-luh koh-MAHNS ah kah-TORZ UR TRAHNT.

responses.french: Il y a un spectacle toutes les deux heures.
responses.english: There's a show every two hours.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah un spek-TAK-luh toot lay duh ZUR.

phrases.text_local: Quel beau cheval ! Il est magnifique.
phrases.pronunciation: kel BOH shuh-VAL ! eel ay mah-nyee-FEEK.
phrases.cultural_note: Horses ('les chevaux') hold an almost sacred place in Kentucky culture, much as they do in Normandy, France — famous for its own thoroughbred breeding. This common ground is a great conversation starter.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the breed of a horse
phrases.text_local: Quelle est la race de ce cheval ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel ay lah RASS duh suh shuh-VAL ?
phrases.cultural_note: Horse breeds are discussed with the same seriousness as wine appellations in Kentucky and Normandy alike. Knowing a few breed names in French — 'Pur-sang' (thoroughbred), 'Arabe' (Arabian) — will impress enthusiasts.

responses.french: C'est un Pur-sang américain, une race connue dans le monde entier.
responses.english: It's an American Thoroughbred, a breed known around the world.
responses.phonetic: say tun poor-SAHN ah-may-ree-KAN, oon RASS koh-NOO dahn luh MOND ahn-TYAY.

responses.french: C'est un Quarter Horse. Très populaire ici au Kentucky.
responses.english: It's a Quarter Horse. Very popular here in Kentucky.
responses.phonetic: say tun KWAHR-tur HORS. tray po-poo-LAIR ee-SEE oh ken-TUH-kee.

phrases.intent_en: Buy a ticket at the entrance
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais un billet adulte, s'il vous plaît. C'est combien ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DRAY un bee-YAY ah-DOOLT, seel voo PLAY. say komb-YAN ?
phrases.cultural_note: In France, admission tickets are 'des billets' — the same word used for train and concert tickets. Asking the price upfront ('c'est combien ?') is perfectly normal and not considered rude.

responses.french: Le billet adulte est à vingt dollars. Avec accès à tous les spectacles.
responses.english: The adult ticket is twenty dollars. It includes access to all shows.
responses.phonetic: luh bee-YAY ah-DOOLT ay ah van DOL-ar. ah-VEK ak-SAY ah too lay spek-TAK-luh.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Hall of Champions is
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, où se trouve le Hall des Champions ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-KYOO-zay mwah, oo suh TROOV luh HOL day shahm-PYOHN ?
phrases.cultural_note: 'Les Champions' is the same word in French — horse racing legends like Secretariat and Seattle Slew are as revered in French racing circles as in American ones.

responses.french: Continuez tout droit, c'est le grand bâtiment sur votre droite.
responses.english: Keep going straight, it's the large building on your right.
responses.phonetic: kohn-tee-NYOO-ay too DRWAH, say luh grahn bat-ee-MAHN soor votr DRWAHT.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Parade of Breeds
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il y a un défilé de races aujourd'hui ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess keel ee ah un day-fee-LAY duh RASS oh-zhoor-DWEE ?
phrases.cultural_note: The Parade of Breeds is a uniquely American tradition, but the French term 'défilé' (parade or procession) is elegant — it's the same word used for fashion shows in Paris.

responses.french: Oui, le défilé commence à onze heures au manège principal.
responses.english: Yes, the parade starts at eleven o'clock at the main arena.
responses.phonetic: WEE, luh day-fee-LAY koh-MAHNS ah OHZ UR oh mah-NEZH pran-see-PAL.

responses.french: Il n'y a pas de défilé aujourd'hui, mais il y en a demain matin.
responses.english: There's no parade today, but there is one tomorrow morning.
responses.phonetic: eel nyah pah duh day-fee-LAY oh-zhoor-DWEE, may eel ee ahn ah duh-MAN mah-TAN.
